title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Shocking Footage of Baby Pulled Out of Rubble in Venezuela
stories.summary:
Shocking Footage of Baby Pulled Out of Rubble in Venezuela. A baby was miraculously rescued alive from the rubble of a collapsed building in Venezuela on Thursday, one of several amazing rescues that have raised the spirits of survivors and rescue workers as they struggle with the aftermath of the powerful quakes that devastated several Venezuelan cities.

The post Shocking Footage of Baby Pulled Out of Rubble i. Al Jazeera: Father and son pulled out alive four days after Venezuela earthquake.

Al Jazeera reports that ootage shows search and rescue workers pulling out father and son from under the rubble of a collapsed building.

links.excerpt: ootage shows search and rescue workers pulling out father and son from under the rubble of a collapsed building.

stories.title: At least 117 dead dogs found in 'horrific scene' at California 'no-kill' shelter
stories.summary:
At least 117 dead dogs found in 'horrific scene' at California 'no-kill' shelter. The remains of at least 117 dogs were found on the grounds of a California "no-kill" animal shelter, many of them with gunshot wounds, authorities said.

BBC: 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' animal rescue - many with gunshot wounds.

BBC reports that hundreds more animals are still missing from the California facility as investigators continue excavating the grounds.

stories.title: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
stories.summary:
Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand. The body of Tunchanok Donhomla, 17, was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.

Al Jazeera: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase.

Al Jazeera reports that simon Peter Carman has been charged with murder after the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was found in a suitcase.

stories.title: Panic Sets In: Dems Tailspin As They Realize The Hostile Socialist Takeover Already Started
stories.summary:
Panic Sets In: Dems Tailspin As They Realize The Hostile Socialist Takeover Already Started. Less than a week after Democratic socialists swept several races in New York’s primaries, the memo appears to have gone out to the more moderate Democrats: the socialists aren’t just coming, they’re already here — and the threat is coming from inside the house.

Three candidates backed by New York City’s Democratic socialist Mayor Zohran ... Fox News: Mamdani claims democratic socialists can win ‘anywhere’ as Democrats feud over party’s future.

Fox News reports that new York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani says democratic socialists can win anywhere in America after endorsed candidates swept Democratic primaries.

stories.summary:

stories.title: Lost your crypto access code? Be wary, there‘s a scam for that too
stories.summary:
Lost your crypto access code? Be wary, there‘s a scam for that too.

A niche type of fraud is lucrative enough for criminals to set up fake websites with dodgy software to harvest your data After holding them for a few years, you have decided it is time to cash in your cryptocurrency holdings.

The problem is, it is so long since you set up the digital wallet which manages them on your laptop, you have forgotten the lengthy access code. Stressed at the thought of losing thousands of pounds, you search and download a program which promises to recover.

stories.title: Maynilad confirms QC condo no longer under residential rate
stories.summary:
Maynilad confirms QC condo no longer under residential rate. MANILA, Philippines — Maynilad Water Services Inc. has confirmed it reclassified Smile Citihomes Condominium’s water account from residential to semi-business in March, resulting in higher water bills for the Quezon City residential complex.

In a statement sent to INQUIRER.net, Maynilad said the reclassification complied with the Metropolitan Waterworks and Sewerage System (MWSS) Regulatory Office’s 2023.

stories.summary:
Man with P13.6-M shabu nabbed in Maguindanao del Norte drug bust. COTABATO CITY – A 42-year-old driver from this city was arrested after authorities seized about two kilograms of alleged shabu worth P13.6 million during a buy-bust operation in Barangay Sarmiento, Parang, Maguindanao del Norte, at 7 a.m. on Saturday.

Edgar Jubay, Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency-Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (PDEA-BARMM) director, identified the suspect. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Lanao del Norte police seize P7.6-M smuggled cigarettes.

stories.summary:

Ministers should end Palantir’s contract before medical confidentiality is sacrificed to Silicon Valley’s appetite for public data Alarm bells ought to have rung when it emerged last month that Palantir engineers could gain “ unlimited access ” to identifiable NHS patient data.

stories.summary: ‘You can’t make billions without hurting people’: Cory Doctorow on Elon Musk, the AI bubble and bosses’ cruel fantasies. The writer who coined the word ‘enshittification’ tells us why AI will never deliver what it promises – and why it still appeals so much to those in power A “centaur”, in automation theory, is a person assisted by a machine, and a “reverse centaur”, hero of Cory Doctorow’s new book, The Reverse Centaur’s Guide to Life After.
